title: × Hesperotropsis leylandii
intro:

The plant I believe you are looking for is Hesperotropsis is actually a nothogenus of the plant family Cupressaceae and is a hybrid of Hesperocyparis and Cupressus (Cupressus macrocarpa).  However, I was unable to find any data about the plant Hesperotropsis,  Hesperotropsis is not recognized by The Plant List, The Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew's Plants of the World online database or other reliable sources.

However, if you are referring to the plant you are actually thinking of is Hesperocyparis, Hesperocyparis is an evergreen tree that produces large, flat, or umbrella-shaped branches. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hesperocyparis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Hesperocyparis is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, although the efficacy and safety of these preparations have not been extensively scientifically studied.
